content.problemStatement:
Chef has two matrices AAA and BBB, each with size N×MN \times MN×M. Each cell of the matrix AAA contains a character '0' or '1', while each cell of the matrix BBB contains '?', '0' or '1'.

The matrix AAA can be modified using zero or more operations. In one operation, Chef can choose two cells in the matrix AAA which lie either in the same row or in the same column and flip the characters in each of these cells (flipping means changing '1' to '0' or '0' to '1').

Chef wants the matrix AAA to match the matrix BBB. Formally, for each row rrr and column ccc, if the cell in row rrr and column ccc of BBB contains '0' or '1', then he wants the cell in row rrr and column ccc of AAA to contain the same character; otherwise (for cells containing '?'), it may contain either '0' or '1'.

The difficulty of your task is described by a parameter EEE. If E=0E = 0E=0, you should only find the smallest number of operations required to achieve this goal. If E=1E = 1E=1, you should also find one sequence of operations with the smallest length which achieves it.
constraints: for each valid iii, AiA_iAi​ contains only characters '0' and '1'
for each valid iii, BiB_iBi​ contains only characters '?', '0' and '1'
the sum of N⋅MN \cdot MN⋅M over all test cases does not exceed 200,000200,000200,000
initially, for each pair of side-adjacent cells in the matrix AAA, the characters in them are different (AAA follows a chessboard pattern)
for each valid iii, each character of BiB_iBi​ is '0'
for each valid iii, BiB_iBi​ contains only characters '0' and '1'
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, MMM and EEE.
NNN lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains a single string AiA_iAi​ with length MMM describing the iii-th row of the matrix AAA.
NNN more lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains a single string BiB_iBi​ with length MMM describing the iii-th row of the matrix BBB.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case:


If it is not possible to make AAA match BBB, print a single line containing the integer −1-1−1.
Otherwise, first, print a line containing a single integer KKK ― the smallest required number of operations.
Then, if E=1E = 1E=1, print KKK lines describing the sequence of operations you want to perform. An operation should be printed in one of the following formats:

`R r i j` to flip the characters in cells in row rrr and columns iii and jjj (1≤r≤N1 \le r \le N1≤r≤N, 1≤i,j≤M1 \le i, j \le M1≤i,j≤M)
`C c i j` to flip the characters in cells in column ccc and rows iii and jjj (1≤c≤M1 \le c \le M1≤c≤M, 1≤i,j≤N1 \le i, j \le N1≤i,j≤N)


If there are several possible answers, you may find any one of them.
